justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains four persons and six vehicles, so it is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attribute labels (atomic action, simple context, age, motion, trunk/doors) for the reported entities but omits most actors and many annotations present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The truck is correctly noted as blocking part of the road and a pedestrian is placed left of the truck, but spatial relations for the other actors are missing or unreported.
justifications.overall: Because many objects are omitted despite some accurate attribute and relational labels for reported entities, the overall extraction quality is low.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures one pedestrian and one vehicle but omits a second pedestrian and four of the five annotated vehicles, so coverage is poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the reported entities the model provides several attributes (actions, context, age, motion and door/trunk states) but it fails to report these attributes for most ground-truth objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are plausible and relevant for the reported objects but relations are sparse and many objects lack any spatial description.
justifications.overall: The extraction is accurate for the few reported entities but is incomplete across the scene, yielding limited overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Captured only one of four pedestrians and one of five vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a relatively rich set of attributes (actions, context, age, vehicle state) for the detected entities despite missing many entities.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes plausible spatial relations for the reported objects but omits relations for the majority of pedestrians and vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Detailed annotations for the few detected objects are helpful, but low entity recall and missing correspondences result in limited overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one of two pedestrians and one of four vehicles, missing the majority of entities present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the entities it does report the extraction supplies correct fine-grained attributes for the pedestrian and some vehicle properties, but it fails to capture attributes across the many missing vehicles and does not reliably map motion status to the full set of vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided relations (pedestrian left of truck, truck in center) are reasonable and consistent with the scene description but are sparse and fail to describe relations for the other actors.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially accurate for reported entities but is incomplete and sparse overall, yielding a below-average utility for comprehensive scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all three ground-truth pedestrians and only lists two vehicles out of six, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the model provides vehicle types, motion status, and door/trunk states, it mislabels at least one vehicle type, omits many vehicles entirely, and provides no pedestrian attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction supplies a couple of relative locations ('to the left of the ego vehicle', 'in front of the silver truck') but relations are sparse, not verified against ground truth, and lack coverage for other objects.
justifications.overall: Overall the output contains some useful semantic details for a couple of vehicles but fails to detect pedestrians, misses most vehicles, and provides limited and partially unreliable spatial information.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 4 pedestrians and 2 vehicles while ground truth lists 4 persons and 6 vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes reported for the detected entities (action, age, motion) match some ground truth items but many contextual labels (jaywalking, carrying) and several vehicle states are missing or ambiguous.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided a few pairwise relations (near the sidewalk, next to/in front) that are plausible but sparse and do not cover the majority of scene relationships or precise road versus sidewalk placement.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures a small subset of correct attributes and relations but is incomplete and missing key actors and context, yielding a below-average result.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 vehicles and no pedestrians while the ground truth contains 5 vehicles and 3 people, so major entity-level omissions occur.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides useful attributes for the reported vehicles (type, motion_status, trunk/doors) but misses person attributes entirely and lacks object IDs linking to ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial phrases are present for the two reported vehicles, but most objects (including all pedestrians) lack spatial context and relations cannot be verified against the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures some vehicle-level details but omits most entities (notably all pedestrians) and many spatial/contextual links, yielding low overall coverage and utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 6 pedestrians and 2 of 7 vehicles with many missing tracks and attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes like action and age and vehicle door/trunk states but omits many ground truth contextual labels such as carrying, talking on phone, jaywalking, and multiple vehicle instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a few spatial relations but they are sparse, include unverifiable or mismatched references, and do not cover the majority of objects in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partial and somewhat accurate for the few detected entities but is missing most pedestrians and vehicles and contains incomplete or imprecise contextual and spatial information, limiting practical usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Extraction captures only one of seven pedestrians and two of five vehicles, missing most objects and therefore is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides some attributes (actions, age, motion) but misses many ground-truth contextual labels (jaywalking, carrying, talking) and per-object IDs, so its semantic detail is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are present but often imprecise or inconsistent with ground-truth contexts (e.g., claiming a pedestrian is at a crossing when ground truth labels jaywalking), showing low spatial accuracy.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction contains some useful high-level info but misses most actors and many important semantic and spatial details, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of five pedestrians and two of four vehicles, missing several actors and many annotated attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (age, clothing hint, simple context) but assigns incorrect atomic action and omits or misreports many communicative and transporting details present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes basic left/middle relations that may be plausible, but relations are sparse, incomplete, and do not capture positions for most actors or distinguish parked vs moving accurately for all vehicles.
justifications.overall: Low overall accuracy due to many missed actors and incorrect actions despite offering some useful attribute hints and limited spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: Detected only 2 of 7 pedestrians and 1 of 4 vehicles, missing the majority of objects and many per-object annotations.

justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several plausible spatial relations for the few detected agents but contains inaccuracies (e.g., labeling someone as using a pedestrian crossing when ground truth indicates walking along the road or jaywalking) and lacks relations for most objects.
justifications.overall: Offers useful labels for a couple of entities but overall performance is limited due to many missed objects and some incorrect attribute/context assignments.

justifications.completeness: The extraction contains only 1 of 7 pedestrians and 2 of 3 vehicles (3 of 10 ground truth objects), so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are correct such as pedestrian atomic action and age, but several important labels are wrong or missing (incorrect vehicle type, missing parked status, incorrect communicative and transporting details).
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are vague or unsupported and do not capture many relevant relations from the scene, and vehicle relative placement and parked relation are incorrect or incomplete.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction shows limited coverage and several incorrect or missing semantic details, yielding low reliability for driving perception use.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of seven pedestrians and two of four vehicles, missing most people and parked vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted entities include useful attributes like actions, context, age, motion and door/trunk states, but many scene actors and context details are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided and include relative position between vehicles, but they are sparse and not verifiable against ground truth which lacks spatial labels.
justifications.overall: Extraction provides accurate labels for a few entities but is incomplete overall, yielding moderate utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 of 5 ground-truth persons and 2 of 5 ground-truth vehicles, so it is highly incomplete.

justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided but they are sparse, not consistently present for all objects, and not tied to ground-truth ids making alignment unclear.
justifications.overall: While some reported objects have correct attributes, the large number of missing entities and incomplete alignment to ground truth produce a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only 2 pedestrians and 1 vehicle while the ground truth contains 6 persons and 5 vehicles, so most objects are missing.

justifications.completeness: Extraction reports 2 of 5 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles from the ground truth, missing the majority of objects.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian and one vehicle were reported while the ground truth lists four persons and six vehicles, so coverage is very low.

justifications.overall: Low entity coverage combined with partially accurate attribute and spatial information results in a below average overall extraction quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only two pedestrians and one vehicle while the ground truth lists six pedestrians and four vehicles, so most objects are missing.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only 2 of 4 pedestrians and 2 of 5 vehicles and omits key attributes such as one pedestrian's communicative state and several vehicle motion statuses.

justifications.completeness: Extraction covers only two pedestrians and one vehicle while ground truth contains seven pedestrians and four vehicles, so many objects are missing.

justifications.completeness: Only one of four pedestrians and one of two vehicles from the ground truth were detected, so many objects are missing.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one of six annotated pedestrians and one of two vehicles, so most objects are missing.
